text:
PET-CT SCAN WITH DIAGNOSTIC CONTRAST-ENHANCED CT
WHOLE BODY (SKULL BASE TO MID-THIGH) WITH IV CONTRAST

INDICATION:
62-year-old female, East Asian descent, former smoker (20 pack-year history, quit 12 years ago), with known history of hypertension, well-controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus, and GOLD Stage I COPD. Presenting symptom: progressive cough and mild exertional dyspnea of several weeks' duration. Chest imaging (outside CT) identified a right upper lobe mass. Referred for oncologic staging PET-CT. Clinical query: extent of metabolically active disease; presence of regional or distant metastases.

RADIOPHARMACEUTICAL:
18F-fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG), 10.2 mCi administered intravenously. Uptake period: 63 minutes. Blood glucose at time of injection: 108 mg/dL. Images acquired from skull base to mid-thigh using integrated PET-CT platform (Siemens Biograph mCT). Diagnostic-quality CT performed with IV contrast (iohexol 350 mg I/mL, 90 mL, power injected, portal venous phase). Oral contrast administered for bowel opacification.

TECHNIQUE:
Attenuation-corrected PET images were reconstructed and co-registered with the contrast-enhanced CT. Images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes on dedicated PET-CT workstation. Maximum intensity projections (MIP) reviewed in conjunction with cross-sectional imaging.

COMPARISON:
Prior outside CT chest (outside institution, obtained approximately 3 weeks prior). No prior PET imaging available for comparison.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No intracranial imaging included in this study. Dedicated brain MRI has been ordered separately. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy with abnormal FDG uptake identified. No fluorodeoxyglucose-avid lesions in the neck, oropharynx, or thyroid. A small right level IIA lymph node measures 0.7 cm in short axis and is not hypermetabolic. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the head or neck regions.

THORAX:

Primary Mass -- Right Upper Lobe:
There is a dominant, intensely hypermetabolic mass in the posterior right upper lobe, centered near the origin of the posterior segmental bronchus of the right upper lobe. The lesion measures 4.8 cm in its greatest transaxial dimension (approximately 4.8 x 4.1 x 3.9 cm in three planes). The margins are markedly spiculated, with linear spicules radiating peripherally into the surrounding parenchyma, consistent with a desmoplastic or infiltrative neoplastic process. There is direct abutment of the right upper lobe bronchus, though frank endobronchial invasion is difficult to exclude based on CT morphology alone. No post-obstructive atelectasis or consolidation is identified distal to the lesion. The mass demonstrates intense and heterogeneous FDG uptake with a measured SUVmax of 14.2 and SUVmean of 8.6 (3D volume of interest). A small central focus of relative photopenia may reflect central necrosis, though this is subtle. No satellite nodules in the right upper lobe. The lesion does not appear to contact the pleura at this level, though the interface with the mediastinal pleura posterolaterally is close. The chest wall is not invaded.

Mediastinum and Hilar Lymph Nodes:
There is multifocal hypermetabolic right mediastinal lymphadenopathy involving at minimum two nodal stations, as detailed below:

-- Right paratracheal lymph node, station 4R: A conglomerate of enlarged right paratracheal lymph nodes is present, with the dominant node measuring 1.8 cm in short axis on CT. The FDG activity is markedly elevated, with an SUVmax of 8.7. The nodes lie immediately posterior to the superior vena cava and anterolateral to the trachea. There is mild mass effect on the adjacent tracheal wall but no luminal narrowing or displacement.

-- Subcarinal lymph nodes, station 7: An enlarged subcarinal lymph node cluster is identified at the carina, measuring up to 1.5 cm in short axis on CT. These are hypermetabolic with an SUVmax of 6.4. The esophagus is not displaced and appears patent. The main bronchi are not compressed.

-- Right hilar lymph nodes, station 10R: There are mildly enlarged right hilar lymph nodes (up to 1.1 cm short axis) with borderline FDG uptake (SUVmax 2.8), which may represent reactive changes versus low-level involvement; this is indeterminate and cannot be definitively characterized by metabolic activity alone.

-- Left-sided mediastinal nodes (stations 4L, 5, 6): No abnormally enlarged or hypermetabolic lymph nodes identified on the contralateral mediastinum. No evidence of contralateral N3 disease.

-- No abnormal axillary lymph nodes with FDG uptake bilaterally.

Lungs and Airways:
The remaining right lung parenchyma demonstrates mild background heterogeneity consistent with the patient's known COPD, including mild centrilobular emphysema most pronounced in the upper lobes bilaterally. No additional pulmonary nodules in the right middle or lower lobes. No ground-glass or consolidative changes suggesting pneumonia or synchronous primary. The left lung is clear without focal parenchymal abnormality, consolidation, or nodules of concern. No endobronchial lesion identified in the left lung tree. No bronchiectasis.

Pleural Spaces and Pericardium:
No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pleural thickening or pleural nodularity. No pericardial effusion. The pericardium is unremarkable.

Heart and Great Vessels:
The cardiac chambers are normal in size. Mild coronary artery calcification is noted, in keeping with the patient's age and cardiovascular risk factors. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber and contour without aneurysmal dilatation or intraluminal filling defect. The pulmonary artery is not enlarged. No mediastinal widening.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and morphology, demonstrating homogeneous parenchymal attenuation on contrast-enhanced CT without focal hypodense or hyperdense lesion. There is no abnormal hepatic FDG uptake above physiologic background. No hepatic mass, cyst, or lesion suspicious for metastatic involvement. The intrahepatic and extrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The portal vein is patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
The gallbladder is present and unremarkable without cholelithiasis or wall thickening. The common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen:
Normal size and morphology. No focal splenic lesion. No splenic FDG uptake above physiologic level.

Pancreas:
The pancreas is of normal caliber with no ductal dilatation, focal mass, or parenchymal abnormality. No hypermetabolic focus in the pancreatic head, body, or tail.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology. No adrenal nodule or mass identified. No abnormal FDG uptake in either adrenal gland. This is particularly relevant given the known clinical concern for metastatic lung adenocarcinoma, as adrenal metastases are common in this setting; none are present.

Kidneys and Ureters:
The kidneys are symmetric in size and demonstrate normal cortical thickness and enhancement. There are bilateral punctate cortical hypodensities too small to characterize, likely simple cysts or tiny foci of scarring; these are non-hypermetabolic. Renal pelves and ureters are not dilated. No obstructive uropathy.

Gastrointestinal Tract:
The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are unremarkable without focal wall thickening, mass, or hypermetabolic mucosal lesion. Physiologic FDG uptake in the bowel wall is noted. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

Peritoneum and Retroperitoneum:
No ascites. No peritoneal or mesenteric nodularity suspicious for carcinomatosis. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy with abnormal FDG uptake. No para-aortic, aortocaval, or iliac chain adenopathy. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber.

Pelvis:
No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Uterus and ovaries not well-evaluated given physiologic activity, however no dominant pelvic lesion identified. Bladder wall is unremarkable. Physiologic urinary tracer excretion in the bladder.

SKELETAL SURVEY:
Axial and appendicular skeleton reviewed on both CT and PET images. No lytic, blastic, or sclerotic lesions suspicious for osseous metastases. No abnormal focal or diffuse skeletal FDG uptake above physiologic marrow background. No pathologic fracture. Degenerative changes noted in the lower thoracic and lumbar spine (mild multilevel facet arthrosis, disc space narrowing at L4-L5), which are not hypermetabolic and consistent with age-appropriate degenerative disease. No rib lesions.

SOFT TISSUES:
No abnormal soft tissue mass or subcutaneous nodularity with hypermetabolic activity. No axillary, epitrochlear, or inguinal adenopathy with FDG uptake.

BRAIN NOTE:
The brain was not included in the PET-CT acquisition field of view, consistent with protocol. A dedicated contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain has been separately ordered for complete intracranial staging.

IMPRESSION:

1. INTENSELY HYPERMETABOLIC RIGHT UPPER LOBE MASS: A dominant spiculated mass measuring 4.8 cm in the posterior right upper lobe demonstrates intense FDG avidity (SUVmax 14.2), morphologically and metabolically highly suspicious for primary malignancy. The imaging appearance is most consistent with primary pulmonary carcinoma. Direct apposition of the right upper lobe bronchus is noted without definitive endobronchial extension on this study.

2. HYPERMETABOLIC RIGHT MEDIASTINAL LYMPHADENOPATHY INVOLVING STATIONS 4R AND 7: Enlarged and markedly FDG-avid right paratracheal (station 4R, SUVmax 8.7) and subcarinal (station 7, SUVmax 6.4) lymph nodes are consistent with ipsilateral mediastinal nodal involvement. These findings are consistent with N2 nodal disease in the context of a right-sided primary lung tumor.

3. INDETERMINATE RIGHT HILAR NODAL ACTIVITY (STATION 10R): Mildly enlarged right hilar lymph nodes with borderline FDG uptake (SUVmax 2.8) are indeterminate; correlation with endobronchial ultrasound-guided sampling is recommended for definitive characterization.

4. NO EVIDENCE OF DISTANT METASTATIC DISEASE WITHIN THE IMAGING FIELD OF VIEW: No hypermetabolic lesions in the liver, adrenal glands, bones, soft tissues, or abdominal/pelvic lymph node chains to suggest distant spread. The adrenal glands are specifically normal and non-hypermetabolic.

5. NO PLEURAL EFFUSION OR PLEURAL NODULARITY.

6. BACKGROUND COPD CHANGES: Mild centrilobular emphysema, upper lobe predominant, without bullous change.

7. BRAIN NOT IMAGED ON THIS STUDY: Dedicated brain MRI pending for intracranial staging.

Overall, the findings are most consistent with a locally advanced right upper lobe primary lung malignancy with ipsilateral mediastinal nodal involvement (stations 4R and 7), without identifiable distant metastatic disease on this examination. The combined morphologic and metabolic profile strongly favors a T3N2M0 staging configuration pending intracranial evaluation. Tissue sampling and correlation with bronchoscopic/EBUS assessment of mediastinal nodal stations is recommended to confirm histology and complete nodal staging.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Nuclear Medicine/PET-CT]
Reviewed by: [Thoracic Radiology Attending]
Report status: Final
